title: Portable identity & credentials for NGOs, volunteers & journalists
problem: NGO workers, volunteers, and journalists operating in hostile environments must repeatedly prove their affiliations to gain access and trust, yet sharing full identity documents puts them at risk of surveillance and retaliation.
solutionSketch: Selective-disclosure credentials backed by onchain verification to manage trust while protecting privacy
targetUser: NGO workers, field volunteers, and journalists in high-risk regions
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic credentials let holders prove affiliation using zero-knowledge proofs — sharing only what's needed, without exposing full identity documents to hostile actors.

title: Credit scoring from verified off-chain financial history
problem: Hundreds of millions of people are creditworthy — they pay rent on time, maintain savings, and run profitable small businesses — but are invisible to formal lenders because their financial history lives in bank apps, rental platforms, and payroll systems that lenders can't access without the borrower surrendering raw account credentials.
solutionSketch: zkTLS-based credit assessments where borrowers prove specific financial claims — 12 months of on-time rent payments, a salary above a threshold, a business revenue history — directly from source platforms without sharing login credentials, giving lenders verifiable underwriting data for borrowers who lack formal credit files.
targetUser: Thin-file borrowers with real financial histories locked in platforms, and lenders wanting to extend credit beyond traditional credit bureau data
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zkTLS proofs cryptographically attest to off-chain financial data at the source — lenders verify claims without seeing raw account data, and borrowers don't need to surrender credentials to a third-party aggregator.

title: Food provenance and traceability
problem: When a food safety recall is issued, tracing contaminated product back to the source takes days or weeks because lot IDs change at every handoff between grower, packer, distributor, and retailer — each maintaining their own disconnected records — meaning contaminated product stays on shelves while investigators manually reconstruct the chain.
solutionSketch: A shared lot ID standard that persists immutably through every handoff in the supply chain — so when a retailer scans a product, they can trace it back to the specific farm field and harvest date in seconds, and a recall can be executed against a precise subset of product rather than pulling entire categories from shelves.
targetUser: Food manufacturers, retailers, regulators, and safety-conscious consumers
whyEthereum: Verifiability: each handoff in the supply chain is recorded as an immutable event — making recalls faster to execute and food fraud detectable by anyone with access to the chain.

title: Farmer finance and parametric insurance
problem: Smallholder farmers in climate-vulnerable regions need weather insurance but manual claims processing takes weeks, requires costly adjusters who can't economically serve small policies, and produces disputes that erode trust in the product. Traditional underwriters also reject farmers who lack formal production records, even when satellite and weather data could substitute.
solutionSketch: Parametric insurance products for farmers triggered automatically by satellite imagery and weather station data — when rainfall or temperature readings cross pre-agreed thresholds indicating crop loss, payouts execute without a claim form or adjuster, making it economically viable to serve smallholders at scale.
targetUser: Smallholder farmers in climate-vulnerable regions without formal credit history
whyEthereum: Enforcement: parametric payouts trigger automatically when oracle-verified data crosses a threshold — making it economically viable to serve small policies that would otherwise require human claims adjustment.

title: Verifiable city climate commitment tracking
problem: Coalitions like C40 Cities and the Global Covenant of Mayors have enrolled hundreds of cities in public emissions reduction pledges — but compliance tracking relies on self-reported data submitted to central secretariats, with no neutral mechanism for member cities or the public to verify whether commitments are being met or to detect when cities quietly abandon targets.
solutionSketch: A shared ledger where city governments record verified emissions data, policy milestones, and adaptation measures — with attestations from IoT sensor networks, third-party auditors, and satellite monitoring — creating a publicly auditable record that any city, funder, or citizen can inspect.
targetUser: Municipal governments in climate coalitions, international climate funders, and citizens holding local governments accountable to public pledges
whyEthereum: Verifiability: emissions records are cryptographically attested by independent data sources and immutably logged — member cities can't retroactively revise their historical commitments, and progress is verifiable by any party without trusting the coalition's secretariat.

title: Common-pool resource management
problem: Emissions caps, fishing quotas, and biodiversity credits are tracked through incompatible national systems that cannot be independently verified, enabling widespread overuse of shared natural resources.
solutionSketch: Verifiable tracking of emissions, fishing quotas, biodiversity credits or water-basin usage across borders
targetUser: International bodies, national regulators, and industries governed by shared resource quotas
whyEthereum: Verifiability: resource usage data from multiple jurisdictions is recorded on a neutral shared ledger — making it possible to detect overuse without relying on any single country's self-reporting.

title: Auditability and compliance for physical systems
problem: Physical systems in manufacturing, logistics, and infrastructure generate safety and maintenance data that is stored in mutable, disconnected databases, making regulatory audits unreliable and tampering undetectable.
solutionSketch: Tamper-evident logs linking physical events to digital records for safety, maintenance, and regulatory reporting
targetUser: Manufacturers, logistics operators, and regulators requiring tamper-evident safety records
whyEthereum: Verifiability: physical events are logged as immutable onchain records — making tampering after the fact detectable and giving regulators a trustworthy audit trail they didn't have to create themselves.

title: Onchain risk pools & reinsurance marketplaces

whyEthereum: Composability: risk pools are transparent and composable — any investor can participate and any insurer can access capacity without navigating opaque relationship-driven markets.

title: Onchain copyright registration
problem: Proving when a creative work was created or who owns it requires navigating slow, expensive government registries that vary by jurisdiction, leaving creators vulnerable to disputes over ownership and unauthorized use.
solutionSketch: Immutable, timestamped proof of creation and ownership for creative works, enabling instant verification of copyright claims across jurisdictions
targetUser: Creators — writers, artists, musicians, photographers — establishing ownership of their work
whyEthereum: Verifiability: a cryptographic timestamp on an immutable ledger proves exactly when a work existed and who registered it — creating evidence of ownership that any court or platform can verify without a government registry.

title: Onchain patent registration and prior art
problem: Inventors lack a tamper-proof way to timestamp lab notebooks, drafts, and experimental data, weakening their ability to establish priority in patent disputes. Formal patent registration is slow, expensive, and jurisdiction-dependent.
solutionSketch: Tamper-proof timestamping and registration for inventions, research data, and prior art — strengthening first-to-invent claims and enabling verifiable patent records
targetUser: Inventors and researchers needing to establish prior art and first-to-invent claims
whyEthereum: Verifiability: lab notebooks, drafts, and experimental data are cryptographically timestamped and immutable — strengthening priority claims in disputes without depending on slow, jurisdiction-specific patent offices.

title: Automated royalty payments and licensing
problem: Royalty payments for patents, music, and other IP are calculated manually through opaque intermediaries, resulting in delayed, inaccurate, or missing compensation. Multi-party licensing agreements involve layers of legal negotiation and manual tracking.
solutionSketch: Smart contracts that meter actual usage and automatically distribute royalties to all rights holders — eliminating intermediary opacity and payment delays for both individual creators and collective licensing arrangements.
targetUser: Musicians, patent holders, authors, and any creator earning from licensed IP
whyEthereum: Enforcement: usage is metered and payments execute automatically at the moment of use — rights holders receive what they're owed without relying on intermediaries to calculate or remit correctly.

whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs let entities demonstrate compliance cryptographically — proving the fact without exposing the underlying data, which is impossible with traditional disclosure frameworks.

title: Verifiable ad delivery and influencer metrics
problem: Ad networks report impressions and clicks using their own unauditable metrics, enabling widespread fraud. Brands also pay influencers based on self-reported or platform-reported reach and engagement that is trivially inflated through bots and fake followers.
solutionSketch: Transparent impression tracking and verifiable reach metrics — with optional zero-knowledge proofs for audience size — so advertisers can independently confirm that spend reached real human audiences.
targetUser: Advertisers, brands, and marketing teams buying digital ad placements and influencer campaigns
whyEthereum: Verifiability: delivery events and engagement signals can be cryptographically attested — giving advertisers independent proof that their spend reached real audiences without relying on the platform's own reporting.

title: Loyalty points as liquid, transferable assets
problem: Frequent flyers and hotel loyalty members collectively hold an estimated $500 billion in unredeemed points — but those points expire silently, can't be combined across programs, can't be sold, and can be devalued unilaterally by the issuing company overnight with no recourse for holders.
solutionSketch: Tokenized loyalty points that are transferable between programs, sellable on secondary markets, and governed by transparent, immutable issuance and expiry rules — so holders have real property rights over value they've earned.
targetUser: Frequent travelers and loyalty program members holding large unredeemed balances, and brands seeking more engaging reward structures
whyEthereum: Composability: loyalty tokens on a shared layer are transferable between any programs that adopt the standard — points combine and flow across brands without bilateral partnership agreements between each issuer.

problem: Warehouse inventory records diverge from reality because tracking systems across suppliers, warehouses, and retailers are disconnected, making financing against inventory unreliable.
solutionSketch: Tokenized SKUs and warehouse receipts that permit programmatic tracking, financing and movement of goods
targetUser: Warehouse operators, manufacturers, and lenders financing inventory
whyEthereum: Verifiability: tokenized inventory creates a single verified record across the supply chain — lenders can confirm collateral exists and hasn't been double-pledged without manual inspection.

title: Open supply-chains
problem: Independent farmers and artisans are excluded from global markets because supply-chain coordination tools are built for large corporations and require expensive integrations they cannot afford.
solutionSketch: Enabling coordination among independent farmers, artisans and open-hardware producers outside corporate platforms
targetUser: Independent farmers, artisans, and small producers excluded from corporate supply chain tools
whyEthereum: Composability: open supply-chain standards mean any producer can plug in without proprietary integrations — coordination happens on a shared layer accessible to small players and large corporations equally.

title: Product certification and circular supply chains
problem: Product origin and certification claims rely on paper trails that can be forged at any handoff, while secondhand buyers have no trustworthy way to verify repair history, authenticity, or remaining lifecycle value.
solutionSketch: Append-only product provenance and certification records that track origin, ownership changes, condition, and end-of-life — supporting both first-sale authenticity claims and circular economy resale.
targetUser: Brands, retailers, regulators, and secondhand buyers seeking verified product history
whyEthereum: Verifiability: every certification claim and ownership transfer is recorded as an immutable event — fraud and greenwashing become publicly detectable rather than just ethically discouraged.

title: Environmental measurement and verification
problem: Environmental claims — RECs, carbon intensity labels, green hydrogen certifications, corporate ESG reports — rely on self-reported data with infrequent third-party audits, enabling double-counting, greenwashing, and eroding trust in sustainability markets.
solutionSketch: Using oracles, remote sensing, IoT devices, and blockchain logs to provide continuous, independently verifiable evidence trails for environmental outcomes including emissions, renewable generation, carbon sequestration, and restoration
targetUser: Corporations making ESG commitments, regulators, and sustainability market participants
whyEthereum: Verifiability: continuous environmental data from IoT sensors and remote sensing is logged immutably — making greenwashing publicly detectable and enabling real-time rather than annual reporting.

title: Transparent carbon and biodiversity credit markets
problem: Carbon credits and biodiversity offsets are traded as undifferentiated commodities despite vast differences in methodology and verification rigor, allowing low-quality credits to crowd out legitimate ones. Forests, watersheds, and biodiversity also lack liquid market representations.
solutionSketch: Differentiated environmental asset classes with onchain provenance, methodology metadata, and verification quality — enabling transparent pricing of ecological assets and markets that reward genuine impact.
targetUser: Carbon market participants, conservation organizations, and corporations with net-zero commitments
whyEthereum: Verifiability: provenance and methodology are cryptographically attached to each credit — buyers can verify quality without relying on opaque rating agencies, and double-counting is publicly detectable.

solutionSketch: Tracking recycling, reuse, product-life-cycles and certification of materials in a transparent ledger
targetUser: Manufacturers, recyclers, and regulators verifying circular economy claims
whyEthereum: Verifiability: recycled material provenance is recorded as an immutable chain of custody — circular claims are cryptographically verifiable rather than self-reported.

title: Digital vehicle passports
problem: A vehicle's full history — manufacturing specs, ownership transfers, lien status, accident damage, and maintenance — is scattered across dealer records, insurance databases, and government registries that do not communicate, making title fraud and odometer rollback easy.
solutionSketch: End-to-end, tamper-evident records covering manufacturing, ownership, liens, maintenance, accidents, and recycling — serving as both lifecycle passport and verifiable title
targetUser: Car buyers, insurers, lenders, and regulators assessing vehicle history and title
whyEthereum: Verifiability: a vehicle's full lifecycle — manufacturing, ownership, maintenance, accidents — is recorded as an immutable chain, making title fraud and odometer rollback publicly detectable.

whyEthereum: Composability: interoperable energy credit settlement on a shared layer means EV drivers use any network and grid credits flow between providers — without bilateral integration agreements.

title: Spot freight market for trucking
problem: Freight brokers sit between shippers who need loads moved and carriers who have trucks available, taking 15–20% margins on every transaction in exchange for making the match — a spread that exists almost entirely because the broker holds the relationship and information asymmetry, not because they add logistical value that a transparent protocol couldn't replace.
solutionSketch: An open spot freight marketplace where shippers post loads with specifications and payment terms, carriers bid directly, payment is escrowed onchain and released automatically upon verified delivery, and both parties build a public reputation from their transaction history — cutting out the broker layer on transactions that don't require physical coordination.
targetUser: Independent truckers and small carriers looking to fill capacity, and shippers who move freight regularly and pay broker margins they know are unnecessary
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payment is escrowed and releases automatically when GPS-verified delivery is confirmed — neither party needs to trust the other or a broker to hold funds honestly, and the reputation system is portable across any platform that reads the same ledger.

solutionSketch: Self-sovereign identities for drivers, vehicles, and riders that work across operators, cities, and borders
targetUser: Drivers, vehicle operators, and riders who cross operator, city, or national boundaries
whyEthereum: Composability: a single onchain credential works across any operator or jurisdiction that accepts the standard — no re-verification required at each border or network boundary.

solutionSketch: Shared sensor and telematics data layers that support traffic optimization, and planning
targetUser: City planners, transit operators, and mobility service providers
whyEthereum: Composability: sensor and telematics data shared on an open layer is accessible to any city service without proprietary integrations — enabling coordinated traffic management across operators.

title: Prediction markets for forecasting
problem: Governments, businesses, and researchers rely on polls, surveys, and expert panels to forecast elections, policy outcomes, and economic events — mechanisms with no accountability for being wrong and no way to aggregate the dispersed knowledge held by the people closest to the situation.
solutionSketch: Open markets where participants buy and sell positions on the outcome of real-world events, with payouts executing automatically when oracle-verified results are confirmed — turning forecasting into a mechanism that rewards accuracy and aggregates distributed knowledge.
targetUser: Policy researchers, businesses making strategic bets, and forecasters wanting a mechanism to profit from being right
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payouts execute automatically when oracle-verified outcomes are confirmed — no bookmaker needed to hold funds or settle positions, and no ability to manipulate results after the fact.

title: Migrant worker remittances
problem: A Kenyan nurse working in London sending $200 home loses $12–20 to Western Union and correspondent bank fees — more than the entire daily wage of the family member receiving it — while the transfer takes 1–3 days and requires both parties to have access to physical agents.
solutionSketch: Stablecoin transfers that move value internationally in minutes at near-zero marginal cost, with recipients cashing out through local mobile money networks or holding value in digital wallets without needing a bank account.
targetUser: Migrant workers sending regular transfers home to family members in emerging markets
whyEthereum: Composability: stablecoins settle on a shared global layer without correspondent banking infrastructure — sender and recipient transact directly regardless of jurisdiction, with no intermediary banks extracting spread on each hop.

title: End-to-end verifiable voting
problem: Paper ballots are manually counted by fallible humans in processes that are hard to audit at scale, while existing e-voting systems ask voters to simply trust that a proprietary black box counted their vote correctly — with no way for individual voters to verify their ballot was included without the system's operator confirming it.
solutionSketch: Voting infrastructure using zero-knowledge proofs that lets each voter verify their individual ballot was included in the final tally, while keeping votes secret and making the overall count auditable by any independent observer without requiring trust in any single party.
targetUser: Election commissions, municipal governments, and large organizations running binding votes
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs allow voters to cryptographically confirm their ballot was counted correctly without revealing who they voted for — combining ballot secrecy with individual verifiability for the first time.

title: Shell company and beneficial ownership registries
problem: The true owners of companies, real estate, and assets can be hidden behind layers of anonymous shell companies across multiple jurisdictions — a structural feature exploited for money laundering, sanctions evasion, and tax fraud — while existing beneficial ownership registries rely on self-reporting with no verification mechanism.
solutionSketch: Cryptographically attested beneficial ownership records that chain legal entities to verified ultimate owners across jurisdictions, allowing regulators and financial institutions to verify real ownership without relying on self-filed declarations.
targetUser: Financial compliance officers, anti-corruption investigators, and regulators tracking cross-border asset ownership
whyEthereum: Verifiability: ownership attestations are cryptographically signed and immutable across jurisdictions — false filings become detectable and cross-border verification happens on a shared neutral ledger rather than through slow bilateral regulatory cooperation.

title: Proof of personhood
problem: Any digital system that gives one vote, one reward, or one allocation per person is immediately gamed by whoever can create the most fake accounts — airdrops get drained by bots, votes get bought in bulk, and social platforms are flooded by coordinated inauthentic behavior — because there is no cheap, privacy-preserving way to prove an account belongs to a unique real human.
solutionSketch: Systems that establish a cryptographic guarantee that an account belongs to a unique living person — without revealing who that person is — enabling sybil-resistant token distributions, one-person-one-vote governance, and spam-free social platforms.
targetUser: Protocols distributing tokens fairly, organizations running one-person-one-vote governance, and platforms trying to keep bots out
whyEthereum: Verifiability: personhood attestations are cryptographically issued and publicly checkable by any application — platforms verify uniqueness without storing biometric data or trusting a single identity provider that becomes a centralization point.

title: Age and eligibility proofs without identity disclosure
problem: A teenager trying to prove they're 18 to access a website, or a doctor proving they hold a medical license to prescribe remotely, must hand over their full government ID — exposing date of birth, address, and ID number to a third party who only needed a yes/no answer — creating surveillance infrastructure and breach risk for a simple eligibility check.
solutionSketch: Zero-knowledge proofs that let individuals demonstrate they meet a threshold — over 18, licensed physician, accredited investor — without revealing the underlying document, name, or any other personal attribute beyond the specific claim being verified.
targetUser: Adults accessing age-restricted services, licensed professionals verifying credentials remotely, and platforms required by law to verify eligibility
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs confirm eligibility cryptographically without exposing underlying identity — platforms get regulatory compliance without building a database of sensitive documents that becomes a breach target.

title: Clinical trial pre-registration and results registry
problem: Pharmaceutical companies register clinical trials, then selectively publish only the results that support their drug — a practice called outcome reporting bias that systematically distorts the medical evidence base and leads doctors to prescribe treatments that don't work or cause harm, because negative results simply disappear.
solutionSketch: An immutable registry where trial protocols and primary endpoints are cryptographically committed before a trial begins — making it publicly provable whether published results match what was pre-registered and impossible to quietly bury unfavorable outcomes.
targetUser: Medical researchers, journal editors, regulators approving drugs, and clinicians making prescribing decisions
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic timestamps prove exactly what was pre-registered and when — selective reporting becomes publicly detectable rather than just ethically discouraged, because the original commitment cannot be altered retroactively.

title: Affordable housing covenant enforcement
problem: Affordable housing deed restrictions — requiring units to remain below market rate for 30, 50, or 99 years — are written into paper legal documents that must be manually checked at every ownership transfer, and routinely slip through conveyancing processes, effectively converting affordable units to market rate with no enforcement mechanism.
solutionSketch: Affordability restrictions encoded directly into property tokens that enforce covenants automatically at the point of transfer or rental — making it structurally impossible to sell or rent above the allowed price without the covenant being triggered.
targetUser: Municipal housing agencies, community land trusts, and affordable housing developers needing long-term covenant enforcement
whyEthereum: Enforcement: affordability rules are baked into the asset itself and execute at every transfer — covenant compliance becomes a property of the token, not a legal obligation that requires someone to notice and litigate a violation.

title: Disaster relief fund tracking
problem: After a major disaster, dozens of NGOs, government agencies, and informal groups respond simultaneously with no shared view of who has received what — aid duplicates in accessible areas while remote communities receive nothing, and donors have no way to verify whether the funds they gave reached affected people rather than being absorbed by overhead.
solutionSketch: Shared infrastructure for real-time tracking of aid disbursements, supply distribution, and beneficiary verification across all responding organizations — with donors able to trace contributions from receipt to final delivery to verified individuals.
targetUser: Humanitarian NGOs coordinating in the field, institutional donors requiring accountability, and affected communities receiving aid
whyEthereum: Verifiability: every disbursement is recorded on a shared immutable ledger accessible to all parties in real time — gaps and duplications are visible as they happen, rather than discovered in post-crisis audits months later.

title: Digital letters of credit
problem: A letter of credit — the bank guarantee that an importer will pay an exporter on delivery — still requires physical paper documents to be couriered between banks across countries, a process that takes 5–10 days and costs $500–1500 per transaction for what is fundamentally a message saying 'we guarantee payment if these conditions are met.'
solutionSketch: Smart contract-based letters of credit that issue, transfer, and settle digitally — releasing payment to the exporter automatically when shipping documents and delivery conditions are cryptographically verified, without physical document exchange.
targetUser: Exporters in emerging markets waiting weeks for payment, and the banks intermediating international trade finance
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payment releases automatically when verifiable conditions are met cryptographically — the 5–10 day physical document courier process collapses to minutes because the document and the payment obligation are the same object.

title: Water rights trading and usage verification
problem: In drought-prone regions, water rights are traded in informal bilateral markets with no price transparency, while actual usage is monitored by infrequent physical inspections that miss illegal diversions — creating a tragedy of the commons where overuse is profitable and nearly undetectable until aquifers are depleted.
solutionSketch: Tokenized water rights tradeable on transparent markets, with IoT-verified usage continuously recorded onchain — creating a real-time allocation registry where overuse is immediately visible and rights can be transferred without paper title processes.
targetUser: Agricultural water users, municipalities, water utilities, and regulators managing allocation in water-stressed basins
whyEthereum: Verifiability: actual water usage attested by IoT sensors is recorded immutably — illegal diversions become publicly detectable as deviations from onchain allocations, rather than discovered only when downstream users notice reduced flow.

title: Community broadband infrastructure financing
problem: Rural and underserved communities lack broadband because incumbent telecoms won't build where returns are uncertain, while community-owned networks and municipal ISPs can't access the upfront capital needed to deploy infrastructure — leaving residents dependent on monopoly providers or no connectivity at all.
solutionSketch: Tokenized financing for community broadband infrastructure — letting local residents, municipalities, and impact investors co-fund deployment and receive proportional returns from subscription revenues, with usage and revenue data transparently reported onchain.
targetUser: Rural communities without viable broadband options, municipal governments, and impact investors funding connectivity infrastructure
whyEthereum: Composability: infrastructure ownership, revenue distribution, and usage reporting live on a shared layer — many small investors can participate in a community network without bespoke legal structures for each funding arrangement.

title: Verifiable biodiversity credits
problem: Corporations making 'nature-positive' commitments buy biodiversity credits from projects measured by inconsistent methodologies and audited infrequently — a market where a credit representing habitat that was degraded a year after issuance looks identical to one representing thriving ecosystem, enabling systematic greenwashing with no accountability.
solutionSketch: Biodiversity credits with continuous verification using satellite imagery, acoustic monitoring, and IoT sensors — with ecological outcomes recorded immutably onchain and credits automatically flagged or invalidated if verified habitat outcomes reverse.
targetUser: Conservation organizations generating credits, corporations with biodiversity net-gain commitments, and regulators overseeing nature markets
whyEthereum: Verifiability: ecological outcomes are continuously attested using remote sensing data logged immutably — a credit's ongoing validity is cryptographically provable rather than dependent on infrequent audits that can't detect degradation between visits.

title: Creator rights and compensation for AI training data
problem: AI models are trained on billions of creative works scraped from the internet without consent or compensation — writers, artists, and photographers whose work shaped these models have no mechanism to verify whether their work was included, no ability to opt out retroactively, and no way to claim revenue from models that directly replicate their style or content.
solutionSketch: Cryptographic provenance records for training datasets that allow creators to register their work, verify inclusion in training corpora, and receive automatic compensation from model operators under programmable licensing terms set at registration.
targetUser: Writers, visual artists, musicians, and photographers whose creative output is used as AI training data
whyEthereum: Enforcement: licensing terms and compensation execute automatically when verified training data usage is confirmed — creators receive proportional payment without requiring AI companies to voluntarily disclose what they trained on.

title: Agent-to-agent commerce protocols
problem: As specialized AI agents proliferate — one agent managing calendars, another researching suppliers, another executing trades — they need to hire, pay, and contract with each other autonomously, but there is no standard protocol for agents from different organizations to discover services, agree terms, and settle payments without routing every transaction through a human approval loop.
solutionSketch: Open protocols for AI agents to publish capabilities, negotiate service terms, and settle micropayments autonomously — creating a machine-to-machine service economy where agents from any organization can transact with each other as fluidly as APIs call each other today.
targetUser: Developers building multi-agent pipelines and enterprises deploying networks of specialized AI agents that need to coordinate
whyEthereum: Composability: a shared settlement layer with open standards lets agents from any organization transact without pre-negotiated bilateral integrations — the agent economy becomes interoperable by default rather than requiring every agent developer to build custom payment bridges.

title: Cryptographic source protection for journalists
problem: A government whistleblower who wants to leak to a journalist faces a dilemma: submitting documents anonymously makes the story hard to verify and easy to dismiss, while providing proof of their insider status — job title, department, access level — makes them identifiable and exposes them to prosecution.
solutionSketch: Zero-knowledge systems where sources prove their organizational affiliation — that they work at a specific agency or hold a specific clearance level — without revealing their identity, generating a cryptographic attestation that journalists can publish to establish credibility without burning the source.
targetUser: Investigative journalists handling sensitive disclosures and insiders at governments or corporations wanting to expose wrongdoing safely
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs confirm a source's credentials cryptographically — the journalist can publish verifiable proof that a source held a specific role without the proof containing any information that identifies who the source is.

title: Harvest pre-financing for smallholder farmers
problem: A subsistence farmer who needs $300 to buy seeds and fertilizer at planting time has no collateral for a bank loan and no trading history with formal lenders — so they borrow from local moneylenders at 60–100% annual interest rates, or go without inputs and accept a lower yield, trapping them in a cycle where they can never accumulate enough surplus to escape the next season's dependency.
solutionSketch: Pre-harvest financing backed by tokenized future crop receipts — letting farmers collateralize their standing crop against verified planting data, weather feeds, and historical yield records, with repayment automatically deducted from the proceeds when the harvest is sold.
targetUser: Smallholder farmers in emerging markets who need input financing before harvest and lack formal credit history
whyEthereum: Enforcement: repayment executes automatically from harvest sale proceeds when the crop is sold — lenders can extend credit against verifiable onchain collateral rather than paper promises, and farmers don't need to make a separate manual repayment.

title: Last-mile drug authentication
problem: A patient in a Nigerian pharmacy buying antimalarials has no way to verify the drug isn't one of the estimated 1-in-10 medicines in sub-Saharan Africa that are substandard or falsified — counterfeit drugs that contain no active ingredient, the wrong dose, or toxic substitutes kill an estimated 250,000 children annually, and the pharmacist dispensing them often has no way to verify authenticity either.
solutionSketch: Per-unit authentication at the point of dispensing — each package carries a scannable identifier linked to an immutable onchain record of its journey from manufacturer to shelf, allowing any pharmacist or patient with a smartphone to verify an unbroken chain of custody.
targetUser: Pharmacists and patients in regions with high counterfeit drug prevalence, and pharmaceutical manufacturers protecting their brand
whyEthereum: Verifiability: each custody transfer is recorded as an immutable onchain event — a pharmacist scanning a package can verify in seconds whether it passed through legitimate distribution or was inserted into the supply chain fraudulently.

title: Mental health treatment continuity
problem: A patient switching therapists or moving to a new city must reconstruct their entire psychiatric history from memory — what medications worked, what dosages caused side effects, previous diagnoses and treatment plans — because mental health records are kept in individual clinician notes with no standard format and no patient-controlled way to transfer them.
solutionSketch: Patient-controlled mental health records in a standardized, portable format — letting individuals share specific treatment history with new providers while retaining granular control over which details are disclosed and to whom.
targetUser: People with ongoing mental health conditions who change providers, relocate, or seek second opinions
whyEthereum: Composability: a standardized record format on a shared layer means any provider can read a patient's shared history without custom integrations — patients control access through cryptographic keys rather than relying on institutions to forward records.

title: Direct creator storefronts with programmable revenue splits
problem: A musician selling merchandise, a Substack writer selling a course, and an artist selling prints each route payments through 2–4 intermediary platforms that take 10–30% combined, add delays before creators can access funds, and give the creator no way to automatically share revenue with collaborators, producers, or the fans who helped grow their audience.
solutionSketch: Direct storefronts where revenue splits among collaborators, co-creators, and communities execute automatically at the moment of purchase — with instant settlement, programmable royalty flows, and no platform intermediary taking a cut of every transaction.
targetUser: Independent creators selling directly to audiences — musicians, writers, artists, educators — with collaborators or communities to compensate
whyEthereum: Enforcement: revenue sharing terms execute automatically at the moment of sale — collaborators receive their share instantly without waiting for a platform to calculate and remit, and no party can unilaterally change the split.

title: Research reagent and material provenance
problem: Scientific experiments that can't be replicated are often later traced to undisclosed variations in reagents, cell lines, or materials — a batch of antibodies that performed differently from what was published, a cell line that had been unknowingly contaminated — but there's no standard way to record or share the provenance of physical research materials that would let other labs understand what they're actually working with.
solutionSketch: Immutable provenance records for biological materials, reagents, and research tools — tracking origin, handling history, quality control results, and cross-references to experiments that used each lot — so that replication failures can be traced to their source.
targetUser: Research labs attempting to replicate published studies, reagent manufacturers, and journals requiring material disclosure
whyEthereum: Verifiability: provenance records are cryptographically attested at each step of a material's lifecycle — a replication lab can independently verify they're working with the same reagent lot and handling conditions as the original study, rather than trusting self-reported descriptions.

title: Automated air quality enforcement
problem: Cities set air quality thresholds in policy but enforce them manually — when pollution sensors exceed legal limits, triggering traffic restrictions, industrial shutdowns, or emergency funding requires human decisions that are slow, inconsistent, and politically influenced, meaning enforcement reliably lags the pollution events it's supposed to prevent.
solutionSketch: Smart contracts that monitor certified air quality sensor feeds and automatically execute pre-agreed responses when thresholds are breached — issuing traffic restrictions, releasing emergency health funding, or triggering fines against identified polluters — without requiring human sign-off at each enforcement step.
targetUser: City environmental agencies, public health departments, and residents in urban areas with chronic air quality violations
whyEthereum: Enforcement: response logic executes automatically when sensor-verified thresholds are crossed — the gap between a pollution event and its enforcement consequence collapses from days to minutes, and the trigger conditions are publicly auditable rather than subject to regulatory discretion.

title: Community space allocation and revenue sharing
problem: Cities allocate shared spaces — parks, community halls, public plazas — through manual booking systems that have no mechanism to reward responsible users, price spaces dynamically to manage demand, or direct revenue back into the neighborhood rather than into a central municipal budget.
solutionSketch: Token-based booking systems where usage history and community reputation reduce access costs, pricing adjusts to demand, and revenue from commercial bookings flows automatically into a community treasury governed by local residents rather than city hall.
targetUser: Municipal governments managing shared public infrastructure and residents who use and care for local community spaces
whyEthereum: Composability: reputation, booking history, and treasury governance live on a shared layer — any community space in the network can read a user's track record and any resident can participate in treasury governance without a separate account for each venue.

title: Decentralized insurance claims adjudication
problem: Insurance companies face hundreds of disputed claims monthly that don't fit automated processing rules — ambiguous coverage questions, contested damage assessments, conflicting evidence — and route them through traditional arbitration that takes 7–10 days, costs hundreds of dollars per case, and produces outcomes neither party finds credible.
solutionSketch: Specialized juror pools that evaluate disputed insurance claims using standardized evidence packages and structured deliberation protocols — with jurors staking tokens on their verdicts, outcomes determined by stake-weighted consensus, and payment executing automatically once adjudication is complete.
targetUser: Insurers processing high volumes of small disputed claims, and policyholders who feel their claim was wrongly denied or undervalued
whyEthereum: Enforcement: adjudication outcomes execute automatically via smart contract — neither the insurer nor the claimant can delay compliance with a verdict, and the juror incentive structure is enforced programmatically rather than by a professional arbitration body.
